solutions.solution:
(D) Angle traced by minute hand per minute = 6°
∴ Angle traced by minute hand in 2 h 20 min
= [(2 × 60) + 20] × 6°
= (120 + 20) × 6°
= 140 × 6° = 840°

(d) ∴ Angle traced by hour hand per minute = (1/2)°

[Clock diagrams showing 8 O'clock and 2 O'clock]

∴ Angle traced by hour hand in 1 h = 1/2 × 60 = 30°
Time period between 8 O' clock to 2 O' clock = 6 h
∴ Angle traced by hour hand in 6h = 30° × 6 = 180°

(C) ∴ Angle traced by hour hand per minute = (1/2)°

[Clock diagram showing Time = 10 : 35]

Angle traced by hour hand in 10 h 35 min
= [(10 × 60) + 35] × 1°/2
= (635/2) = 317.5°

∴ Angle traced by minute hand per minute = 6°
∴ Angle traced by minute hand in 35 min = 35 × 6° = 210°
∴ Required angle = (317.5° – 210°) = 107.5°
= 107 1/2

(b) ∴ Angle traced by hour hand per minute = (1/2)°

[Clock diagram showing Time = 7 : 10]

∴ Angle traced by hour hand in 7 h 10 min
= (7 × 60 + 10) × 1°/2
= 430 × 1°/2 = 215°

∴ Angle traced by minute hand per minute = 6°
∴ Angle traced by minute hand in 10 min
= 10 × 6° = 60°
∴ Required angle = (215° – 60°) = 155°

(C) ∴ Angle traced by hour hand per minute = (1/2)°

[Clock diagram showing Time = 9 : 30]

∴ Angle traced by hour hand in 9 h 30 min
= [(9 × 60) + 30] × 1°/2
= 570 × 1°/2 = 285°

∴ Angle traced by minute hand per minute = 6°
∴ Angle traced by minute hand in 30 min
= 30 × 6° = 180°
∴ Required angle = (285° – 180°) = 105°
